stylePrompt: storybook illustration, sunrise watercolor, warm gold highlights, soft paper texture

recentDetails.title: Midday transition slowed down after a familiar book
recentDetails.detail: Teacher noted that Xiaoyu initially hesitated before nap, then settled after hearing the first page of a familiar bedtime picture book.

recentDetails.title: Evening separation improved with earlier wind-down
recentDetails.detail: Family reported that moving bath and lights-off earlier reduced the emotional spike before bed and made the bedtime hug shorter.

recentDetails.title: Shared school-home bedtime rhythm is working
recentDetails.detail: The same story-opening and calm breathing sequence helped connect the school nap cue and the home bedtime cue.

recentDetails.title: Preview of tomorrow lowered resistance
recentDetails.detail: Previewing the next morning routine reduced negotiation at home and made morning handoff easier.

ruleFallback.title: Today growth signal
ruleFallback.description: A familiar ritual reduced friction and helped the child settle.

highlightCandidates.title: Today small progress
highlightCandidates.detail: During nap preparation, Xiaoyu was willing to sit down and listen through the first page before relaxing her shoulders.

highlightCandidates.title: What worked in both school and home
highlightCandidates.detail: The familiar story opening plus slow breathing has already been validated in both school and home, so repeating the same rhythm tonight has the best chance to succeed.

highlightCandidates.title: Family bright spot
highlightCandidates.detail: After bedtime was moved slightly earlier, Xiaoyu was more willing to lean in for a short hug and accepted lights-off with less protest.

highlightCandidates.title: Only one thing tonight
highlightCandidates.detail: After bath, read one familiar bedtime book and then do three slow breaths together without adding new requests.

highlightCandidates.title: What to watch tomorrow
highlightCandidates.detail: Observe whether bedtime leaning-in happens earlier and whether morning handoff stabilizes faster.

latestInterventionCard.title: Bedtime regulation loop
latestInterventionCard.summary: Keep the school-home bedtime rhythm aligned and reduce new stimulation tonight.
latestInterventionCard.triggerReason: Bedtime emotion spikes still appear when the sequence changes too much.
latestInterventionCard.tonightHomeAction: After bath, read one familiar bedtime book and then do three slow breaths together without adding new requests.
homeSteps: Turn off stimulating content right after bath and keep only the bedside lamp on.
Read the same familiar bedtime book used in recent calm evenings.
After the last page, say one short sentence about today's bright spot and then breathe together three times.
observationPoints: Whether the body leans in earlier when the book starts
Whether lights-off protest shortens compared with the previous two nights
Whether the next morning handoff settles faster
latestInterventionCard.tomorrowObservationPoint: Watch whether morning handoff stabilizes faster after the same bedtime rhythm.
latestInterventionCard.reviewIn48h: Compare bedtime settling and morning handoff over the next 48 hours before changing the routine again.
latestInterventionCard.parentMessageDraft: Tonight keep the routine small and familiar: one book, three breaths, no new requests.
latestInterventionCard.teacherFollowupDraft: Tomorrow morning check whether the handoff stabilizes faster and whether the same story cue still helps.

latestConsultation.summary: Xiaoyu is already showing that a familiar book and calm pacing can lower bedtime tension; the goal is to preserve that rhythm tonight instead of adding new demands.
latestConsultation.homeAction: After bath, read one familiar bedtime book and then do three slow breaths together without adding new requests.
followUp48h: Observe whether bedtime leaning-in happens earlier and whether morning handoff stabilizes faster.
todayInSchoolActions: Keep the familiar picture book and the same soft voice cue before nap.
tonightAtHomeActions: Use one familiar bedtime book and three slow breaths.
nextCheckpoints: Check whether the child requests the same ritual again.
latestConsultation.reviewIn48h: Compare bedtime settling and morning handoff over the next 48 hours before changing the routine again.
latestConsultation.parentMessageDraft: Tonight keep the routine small and familiar: one book, three breaths, no new requests.

agentFindings.title: Evening arousal is more manageable with predictable pacing
agentFindings.riskExplanation: Unplanned stimulation still increases resistance before sleep.
signals: Bedtime protest fell on evenings with an earlier wind-down
Nap transition improved with the familiar book cue
actions: Preserve the same order of bath, book, breathing, lights-out.

agentFindings.title: Parent accompaniment should stay small and steady
agentFindings.riskExplanation: More instructions at bedtime increases pressure instead of support.
signals: Short, repeated cues worked better than explanation-heavy guidance
actions: Use one sentence after the book, then stop adding requests.
observationPoints: Whether the child stays physically close after the first page

explainability.label: Tonight first step
explainability.detail: Keep the bedtime routine small and familiar.

explainability.label: Next observation
explainability.detail: Look for earlier leaning-in and quicker morning handoff recovery.

evidenceItems.label: Teacher observation
evidenceItems.detail: The familiar story cue helped Xiaoyu settle before nap on 2026-04-07.

evidenceItems.label: Guardian feedback
evidenceItems.detail: Earlier wind-down reduced bedtime protest on 2026-04-06.

evidenceItems.label: Continuity note
evidenceItems.detail: School-home pacing should remain aligned for 48 hours before another adjustment.
